data.content:
The file is generated successfully but not included in compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so dynamically created files are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the dynamically created file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also register it in `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the including-generated-files skill since it's specifically for this scenario.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild's implicit `<Compile>` glob expands at *evaluation* time, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the file at *execution* time, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `<Compile>` (so it's compiled) and `<FileWrites>` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
